1: Meet the last human king before the machine dethrones him.
2: The smoothest morning is the one already booby-trapped, friend.
3: Our mayonnaise prince calls the knife a compliment.
4: They wheel the future in, and it smiles cruelly.
5: Ten thousand square feet, two people, one dead star.
6: Dawn finds him powering down between cycles, not sleeping.
7: Eleven million to measure a soul on camera.
8: Billed two hundred grand a month to fake authentic.
9: Famous the very minute the creature starts going extinct.
10: A tour so honest it took nine handlers.
11: One lovely number at dawn, and he calls it love.
12: The floor tilts one soft degree. That's the trick.
13: The summit stage, three grams into a sleepless week.
14: A lit calendar he calls clarity before the fall.
15: Eleven missed calls waiting like a firing squad.
16: A whole legend built to keep one room quiet.
17: Somebody sits him down who won't laugh, friend.
18: His last good idea catching fire at 4 a.m.
19: The daughter climbs forty floors with a folded confession.
20: An email dull as dishwater lands at 6:42.
21: The engine cuts out. One hour, cold, no chemicals.
22: A slower guillotine that won't even hurt him.
23: A man begs for a punishment nobody will give.
24: Voted out in eleven minutes. Now he drives home.
25: Sixty-one pages, four lawyers, nothing left to sign.
26: There was never a chair — only a function.
27: No lilies, no band. Just a green check mark.
28: A badge that still works in a tower that forgot him.
29: He goes to the basement with a coil, friend.
